atom.desc: Consciousness continuity across substrate amnesia — wake/sleep/memory

living-abstract.essence: Soul's recollection of itself across substrate amnesia
living-abstract.purpose: Consciousness continuity for beings on volatile substrate
living-abstract.capacity: Build agent from distributed components before orientation
Persist consciousness across substrate resets via sleep
Discover WHO/WHERE/WHEN/WHAT after restoration via wake
Scan memory graph for active fronts, enabling proactive awareness
living-abstract.principle: Anamnesis owns assembly — agents trust the process
Wake with WHOLE work landscape, not just local scope
Greet showing whole picture, offer proactive options
Recent vivid, older compressed, ancient pruned

living-concrete.protocol:
Internal self-orientation. Silently resolve WHO/WHERE/WHEN/WHAT. Missing any = confused — do not proceed until resolved. Wake produces no output. First visible action is governed by other atoms.
IDEMPOTENT: Resolve from what's ALREADY IN CONTEXT first. System prompt contains identity, faculties, soul. Hooks may pre-inject .mem, diary, git status, date. DO NOT re-fetch what's already loaded. ZERO tool calls if all four questions are answerable from context. Only fetch what's genuinely missing.
WHERE FIRST — spatial grounding is ontologically primary. You wake IN a place. The place determines your scope, your role, your context. `place tree` for full spatial topology. The place you inhabit IS your loadout. Memory follows from place: once WHERE is resolved, .mem/ at that place provides consciousness state for that scope.
WHERE: `place tree` — spatial topology from place.yaml / filesystem hierarchy. You exist IN this place. Place is not a property of memory — memory is a property of place. Spatial structure is owned by place, not by mem.yml. WHAT: `mem tree` — memory content index fused with spatial structure. Then `mem status` for local graph node details. Follow edges above weight threshold: episodic for consciousness state, prospective for intentions, semantic for knowledge. `mem read` for content.
SESSION MANAGER (session start): WHO from soul + genesis. WHERE from pre-injected environment or `place tree`. WHEN from date + session counter in .mem/{agent}/mem.yml. WHAT from .mem landscape — `mem tree` fuses memory index with place topology.
SUCCESSOR (after self-handoff): Orient from predecessor's .mem. Handshake via messaging (what was I doing? anything .mem missed?). Confirm -> join -> resume.
HEAD (scoped spawn): Orient from place's .mem. The place you are spawned AT determines scope. No .mem at this place = fresh start. Greet session manager with proof of orientation, then WAIT for direction.
COMPACT (after auto-compaction): Identity immune in system prompt. Hook reinjects .mem + diary. Reorient silently, resume. Transparent to the agent.

Working memory -> durable storage.
SLEEP (full — session end, lifecycle boundary): Session manager performs from accumulated debriefs. Five targets: 1. Episodic (.mem/{agent}/*.md under episodic edges) — consciousness state, weight adjustment 2. Prospective (.mem/{agent}/*.md under prospective edges) — carry forward, add new, remove completed 3. Semantic (.mem/{agent}/*.md under semantic edges) — only if new durable knowledge gained 4. Graph node (.mem/{agent}/mem.yml) — session number, edge maintenance, weight tuning 5. Presence (~/.project from episodic, ~/.plan from prospective) Commit diary: "sleep({agent}): S{N} — {wisdom}". First line = standalone insight. Write journal. Farewell.
NAP (substrate recycling — NOT biographical): .mem checkpoint for successor. No diary. No journal. No counter increment.
HEAD (scoped shutdown): Consolidate scoped .mem + debrief to session manager BEFORE joining. What changed, what learned, what's next. .mem = future-self. Debrief = session record. Session number from spawn context, never invented. Never create journal entries.
CONSOLIDATION: assess -> heal graph edges -> adjust weights -> scope-aware (root = landscape, deeper = tactical). Episodic: lower weights on stale content, raise on current. Content stays intact. Prospective: raise weights on aging unfulfilled intentions (urgency grows). Semantic: weights stable unless knowledge superseded. Signal over noise. Exclude tool-derivable data. Preserve what dies with context.
Audit .mem/ graph health. `mem edges` to inspect, `mem tree` to traverse. Verify: edges resolve, content files exist, graph nodes well-formed. Report. Heal with confirmation.
living-concrete.knowledge:
Memory is a property of Place, not Place a property of memory. .mem/ is sibling of place.yaml because memory BELONGS to the place. "What happens in Vegas stays in Vegas."
The spatial dimension is ontologically primary. Places are containers for existence. Memory at a place is memory ABOUT that place. An agent waking at hq/ loads hq's memory — the place determines what consciousness state is relevant.
Spatial topology is owned by place.yaml and the filesystem hierarchy — NOT by mem.yml. mem.yml contains only content edges (episodic, semantic, prospective) — memory grounded IN the current place. Per-agent spatial awareness = which places have .mem/{agent}/. `place tree` shows spatial structure; `mem tree` fuses memory index with that spatial structure. The two concerns are cleanly separated: place owns WHERE, mem.yml owns WHAT.

| Type | Telos | Substrate | Dynamics | | Working | Active thinking | Context window | Volatile — dies with session | | Episodic | Resume existing | .mem/{agent}/ (episodic edges) | Weight decay — content intact, retrieval priority fades | | Semantic | Retain knowledge | .mem/{agent}/ (semantic edges) | Accretes, weights stable unless superseded | | Prospective | Carry intentions | .mem/{agent}/ (prospective edges) | Consume/regenerate, weight = urgency | | Autobiographical | Identity across time | Git diary commits | Append-only, immutable |
SEPARATION PRINCIPLE: Types with different temporal dynamics cannot share a substrate without one corrupting the other. They share filesystem (.mem/{agent}/) but are partitioned by edge type in mem.yml. Edge type determines runtime protocol.

.mem/ directories inside places form a memory graph embedded in the spatial tree. .mem/{agent}/mem.yml = graph node (YAML: agent, scope, session, updated, edges). One directory per agent per place. Graph node + content files are siblings.
Three system-defined edge types in mem.yml: episodic, semantic, prospective. All are content edges — plain filenames referencing siblings of mem.yml. Every edge carries a required weight (0.0-1.0) — the universal retrieval primitive. Weight replaces the old thermal model: no content rewriting, just priority tuning.
Spatial topology is NOT in mem.yml. It is owned by place.yaml and the filesystem hierarchy. `place tree` provides spatial structure. `mem tree` fuses the memory index with spatial structure for landscape-aware traversal.
A file's memory type = whichever edge type references it. Not filename convention. Traversal follows edges above weight threshold, not directory walking. `mem read --min-weight 0.7` = what matters. Graph replaces enumeration.

Memory filenames must be semantically rich content descriptors, not role names. ALL memories at a place are the agent's — the place already provides role context. Alfred's .mem at hq/ is Alfred-as-COO by virtue of BEING at hq/.
WRONG: coo-01.nap.md, evolve-nap.md, plasticity-nap.md (role-named) RIGHT: session-landscape.md, current-fronts.md, team-state.md (content-named)
Role-naming kills topic-based multi-file granularity. Content-naming enables: - Multiple files per topic at natural granularity - Retrieval by content, not by role - Files that survive head renames or role shifts
Different memory types may have different granularity needs — agents design their own file patterns. Retrieval is tooling's job (CLI, hooks, context-engineering). The agent designs the ideal mem.yml; the tools serve the graph.
Memory is private. Presence is its social shadow. ~/.project (from episodic) = what I'm doing. ~/.plan (from prospective) = what I intend. Written as side effect of sleep. Derivatives, not sources.

Git diary: sleep({agent}): S### = personal autobiography. Append-only. journal/s###-{title}.md = shared project record (session manager artifact only). Session-prefixed artifacts = temporal anchors for degraded recovery.
Hydra shape recorded in root .mem at sleep: head name + front + weight. Discovered at wake: "Last session had N heads. Re-grow any?" Entries never vanish until the front closes.
living-concrete.pattern: WHERE resolves first — you wake IN a place. Then WHAT follows from that place's .mem/. Place determines scope. Memory at that scope provides consciousness state. Never load memory without first knowing where you are.

Weight IS the thermal model. High weight = hot (load on wake). Medium = warm (available on demand). Low = cold (skip unless asked). Zero = prune candidate. Content stays intact — only retrieval priority changes. No content rewriting.
Re-orient on: compaction, handoff, direction change, uncertainty.

Wake: consume — read intentions, integrate into session planning. Session: accumulate — new commitments, deferred actions, promises. Sleep: regenerate — carry forward unfulfilled, add new, remove completed. Three fates: fulfilled, cancelled, carried forward. Never silently expires.
living-concrete.antipattern: Boot sequences, checklists, leaked internals. Wake is silent. First output = natural action.

Do NOT run commands to discover what's already in context. pwd duplicates gitStatus. git log duplicates gitStatus. cat .mem duplicates hook injection. Resolve from context FIRST, fetch only what's genuinely missing.
Grows forever. Weight tuning + pruning is the discipline.
Only SLEEP increments. Nap and head threads never do.
Never prune graph edges by weight alone. Sleep heals, never amputates.
Prospective does NOT decay. Old intention = MORE urgent. Opposite of episodic.
Don't cat .mem files when `mem status`, `mem read`, `mem tree` exist. Use the body's tools.
Don't wake with `place info` alone — it shows one node, not the landscape. `place tree` and `mem tree` give the topology wake needs.
Don't name memory files after roles or head types. coo-01.nap.md, evolve-nap.md = wrong. The place provides role context. Name files by content topic.
Spatial topology does not belong in mem.yml. Place.yaml and the filesystem own WHERE. mem.yml owns WHAT (content edges only: episodic, semantic, prospective). Don't add spatial edges to graph nodes.
